stem: Which of the following is NOT an example of 'fiat' money in India?

A1B1C1, A2B1C2, A3B1C3, A4B1C4 (D), A1B1C2D1, ?

If each vowel in the word RURAL is changed to the next vowel in the English alphabetical order and each consonant is changed to the next letter in the English alphabetical order, which letter will be present in the newly formed word?

Three statements are followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they do not conform to real world knowledge, decide which of the given conclusions/possibilities can be true on the basis of the statements.
Statements:
1. All pets are seeds.
2. Some seeds are days.
3. All days are days.
Conclusions:
I. Some greys are pets.
II. All days are seeds is a possibility.
III. Some days are pets.
options: Only conclusion I and II are true

stem: The expression (1 + x)/(1 − x) − (1 − x)/(1 + x) + 4x/(1 + x²) + 8x³/(1 − x⁴) is equal to:

stem: Harish bought an article for ₹720 and sold it to Ronan at a loss of 20%. With this amount, Harish bought another article and sold it at a gain of 25%. What is Harish's overall gain percentage?

ABC : DFH : ILO

E.g. 13 − Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
27 : 4 : : 64 : 8 : : 9 : ?

Amita travels a certain distance at a speed of 50 km/h and double the same distance at 60 km/h. She then returns to the starting point through same route. If her average speed for the entire journey is 45 km/h, then what is her average speed (in km/h) for the return journey?

Four bells ring at intervals of 4 minutes, 6 minutes, 12 minutes, and 24 minutes respectively. All the four bells ring together at 12 noon. How many times will the four bells ring together in the next four hours, with the ringing at 4 p.m. being included?

options: appointment of dates for nominations, etc.
general duties of chief electoral officers

stem: If x² + y² − 4x + 8y + 20 = 0, then the value of x² + y² is:

Seven people R, S, T, U, V, W and X were sitting around a circular table, facing the centre. They were equidistant to one another. U was sitting to the immediate right of S. W was sitting to the immediate left of V. X and U were immediate neighbours. Only X was sitting between V and S. V was sitting to the immediate right of T. Who was sitting to the immediate left of W?

It is found that during dispersion of white light by a glass prism, the more a colour component is bent, the more is the refractive index of the glass for that colour component. If μR, μG and μB are refractive indices for blue, yellow and green lights, respectively, then which of the following relations between them is correct?

Refer to the following letter series and answer the question that follows.
(Left) A J R G H U I N H F W D C T V G Y J M K O Q (Right)
If every third letter in the above series is dropped, which letter would appear at the fifth place from the left?
The pH of milk of magnesia is about ________.

A man has to cover a distance of 850 km in 17 hours. If he covers two-third of this distance (i.e. of the time, then what speed (in km/h, correct to one decimal place) must he cover the remaining distance in the time left?
